exemplar.title: To what extent do the impurities impact the Dielectric constant and capacitance of a circuit?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do the impurities impact the Dielectric constant and capacitance of a circuit?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Title and opening clearly articulate the focus on how impurities affect dielectric constant and capacitance.
Research question is precisely stated and consistently revisited throughout the report.
Variables are well defined and controlled variables are justified, showing strong planning.
weaknesses: Some repetition and minor lapses in diction slightly reduce overall precision.
Key methodological details (number of repeats, multimeter calibration, justification of paint as dielectric) are only briefly addressed.
Researchâquestion statement is truncated in places, which risks ambiguity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates topic and purpose with clarity and links every section back to capacitor performance, meriting a high score in Focus and Method. The research question is focused and continually referenced. Methodological planning is solid, but omitted details prevent a perfect score, so 5 out of 6 appropriately reflects strong but not flawless execution.

strengths: Correct application of the capacitance formula (C = ÎºÎµâA/d) and clear discussion of field distortion.
Consistent use of key terminology (dielectric constant, relative permittivity) demonstrates sound conceptual understanding.
Background theory section effectively links formulae to physical meaning and real-world context.
weaknesses: Incorrect vector notation on scalar quantities (A and d) creates occasional confusion.
Limited discussion of plateâedge effects, permittivity measurement techniques, and quantitative error analysis.
Minor grammatical slips and symbol mislabeling prevent fully consistent use of terminology.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate shows good subject knowledge with mostly accurate terminology and relevant source use. Treatment of advanced aspects (edge effects, uncertainty quantification) is limited, placing the work firmly in the band for Good Knowledge (4â5), so a score of 4 is warranted.

strengths: Data are presented in clear tables and graphs, with trends identified and linked back to theory.
Argument flows logically from hypothesis through results to conclusion, contrasting impurity effects coherently.
Evaluation section recognizes key limitations and proposes realistic improvements (e.g., spray coating, digital gauges).
weaknesses: Statistical analysis is minimalâno uncertainty quantification or error bars.
Evaluation is largely qualitative and informal in tone; critical perspective could be deepened with numerical estimates.
Some claims (dielectric breakdown behaviour) are stated without direct supporting measurements.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research analysis and argument development are both well-executed, demonstrating clear relevance to the question and coherent structure. Critical evaluation is good but remains largely qualitative. This balanced performance places the work in the middle of the Good band (4â6), so 6 out of 12 reflects solid critical thinking without reaching the top band.

strengths: Report follows a clear structure with headings, subheadings, and table of contents in line with conventions.
Figures and tables are legible and support the narrative; word count is declared and within limits.
Layout and schematic diagrams aid comprehension and reinforce key concepts.
weaknesses: Inconsistent table borders and occasional pageâbreak artefacts interrupt visual flow.
Figure captions are sometimes generic or overly verbose; resolution issues reduce professional polish.
Minor formatting lapses (merged words, inconsistent captions) prevent a fully polished appearance.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is clear and generally follows required formatting, with effective visual elements supporting communication. However, minor inconsistencies and polish issues prevent a top score. A mid-band score of 3 out of 4 accurately reflects this level of quality.

strandSelections.reasoning: Key physics termsâdielectric constant, capacitance, relative permittivity, conductive impurityâare used consistently and largely accurately. Occasional grammatical slips (e.g. âelectric field will be distributed uniformlywhichâ) and symbols mislabeled (vector arrows for scalars) prevent full consistency, so a solid 4 is appropriate.

The essay maintains a coherent argumentative thread from hypothesis through conclusion, contrasting conductive and non-conductive behaviours and relating findings to industry.  Structure (introductionâmethodâresultsâevaluation) is logical.  Some reasoning is assertive rather than evidence-based (e.g. claims about dielectric breakdown are not measured), so the work is well-developed but not âsophisticatedâ; score 5.

Method sections list variables, equipment and a step-by-step procedure; diagrams assist comprehension.  However, details such as number of repeats, calibration of the multimeter, uncertainty calculations and justification of paint as a dielectric are only briefly addressed, and literature methods are not critically selected.  Thus methodology is "mostly complete with generally relevant methods" rather than fully appropriate, warranting a high-middle score of 4.

The student applies core electrostatics (C=ÎºÎµâA/d) correctly, discusses field distortion, and references several scholarly sources.  Explanations of conductive versus non-conductive impurity effects show sound conceptual grasp.  Nonetheless, treatment of plate-edge effects, permittivity measurement techniques, and quantitative error analysis is limited, so the work sits securely in the âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ band.

The evaluation section identifies strengths, limitations (thickness variations, impurity settling) and improvement strategies (spray coating, digital gauges).  However, it remains largely qualitative and does not quantify uncertainty or assess impact on conclusions.  Continuous critical reflection is therefore âgoodâ but not embedded âthroughoutâ, meriting the lower end (4) of this band.

The student states the focusââinfluence of impurities on the dielectric constant and capacitanceââin the title, the research-question box, and the opening paragraph. Purposes are explained and there is continuous, explicit linkage back to capacitor performance in electronics, demonstrating clear purpose. Minor lapses in diction and repetition slightly reduce the precision, so âaccurately and effectivelyâ is met, but not to a flawless degree; a mid-top score of 5 is most representative.

Graphs are legible, colour-coded and include axes labels; schematic diagrams reinforce understanding.  Nonetheless, resolution is low and some captions are generic (âFigure 1â), reducing professional finish.  Overall the visual layout supports communication effectively but not flawlessly, justifying a score of 3.

The research question is precisely worded (âTo what extent do impurities affect the dielectric constant and capacitance of a circuit?â) and is re-visited in hypothesis, data presentation and conclusion.  The scope (three impurity types, three concentrations) shows focused intent, but there is some breadth (two response variables, thickness also recorded) that marginally dilutes tightness; therefore 5, not 6, is appropriate.

Data are organised into a clear table and two graphs; trends are identified and linked to theory (charge-dissipation pathways).  While only single averaged values are given and statistical depth is minimal, the analysis remains clearly relevant to the question and interprets differences between impurity types.  This meets âgood analysisâ in the 4-6 band; limited quantitative rigor holds it to 5.

Headings, subheadings, figures, and a table of contents demonstrate clear structure aligned to EE conventions; word count is declared and within limit.  Minor formatting issues (uneven table borders, occasional page-break artefacts, inconsistent figure captions) stop the presentation from being fully polished, so 3 is appropriate within the top band.

annotations.comment: The title clearly states the focus on impuritiesâ effects on dielectric constant and capacitance, demonstrating precise alignment with the research question.
The research question statement appears truncated and trails off; ensure the full question is visible and precisely phrased to maintain focus.
The table of contents is comprehensive, but page numbers are missing; include accurate links between sections and pages for more effective navigation.
The introduction effectively links capacitor performance to the demands of miniaturized electronics, demonstrating strong subject contextualization.
The research question box cuts off mid-sentence; present the full question to avoid ambiguity and reinforce focus.
The hypothesis clearly contrasts conductive versus non-conductive impurities and predicts their effects, showing strong conceptual understanding.
Vector notation is misapplied to plate area and separation; use scalar symbols for A and d in $$C=\kappa \epsilon_0 \frac{A}{d}$$ to avoid confusion.
The background theory section provides clear definitions of dielectric constant and capacitance, effectively linking formula to physical meaning.
The impact of impurities section outlines qualitative effects but lacks quantitative context; reference prior data or include estimates to strengthen analysis.
The figure caption contains a merged word (âuniformlywhichâ); revise for clarity and professionalism.
Variable definitions are clearly presented with independent, dependent, and controlled variables explicitly distinguished.
Thickness measurement is mentioned alongside concentration but the protocol for thickness data integration into calculations is not detailed; specify how d enters uncertainty analysis.
Controlled variables are well justified, demonstrating careful planning to isolate the independent variableâs effects.
The methodology lacks details on calibration of the multimeter and the number of repeated measurements; include these for methodological completeness.
Equipment list is comprehensive but omits model numbers and measurement tolerances; specify these to allow reproducibility.
Justify the use of paint as a dielectric medium by citing material properties or literature to strengthen methodology rationale.
The capacitor construction diagram is clear, but its caption is overly verbose; condense to essential details with clear labels.
Procedure overview is concise but omits the number of trials averaged; include this to clarify data reliability.
The mixing protocol mentions homogeneity but lacks verification; describe how mixing quality was assessed (e.g., visual inspection, consistency tests).
The data table is neatly structured and includes all variable categories, supporting clear trend identification.
Graphical representation effectively shows negative correlation between impurity concentration and capacitance, supporting the hypothesis.
The evaluation section outlines general strengths but the language (âpainfully controlledâ) is informal; revise tone for academic consistency.
Limitations discussion identifies key issues but remains qualitative; quantify the impact of thickness variation on capacitance where possible.
Works cited list is extensive but citation styles vary; apply a consistent referencing format (e.g., APA or IEEE) throughout.
